'''''UFC Freedom 250''''' (also known as '''''UFC White House''''' and '''''UFC at the White House''''')<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.paramountplus.com/sneak-peak/where-to-watch-white-house-ufc/|title=UFC at the White House: Where to watch|publisher=paramountplus.com|author=Christopher Cason|date=2026-05-15|access-date=2026-05-17|archive-date=May 21, 2026|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20260521174342/https://www.paramountplus.com/sneak-peak/where-to-watch-white-house-ufc|url-status=live}}</ref> was a [[mixed martial arts]] event produced by the [[Ultimate Fighting Championship]] that took place on June 14, 2026, on the [[South Lawn]] of the [[White House]] in [[Washington, D.C.]], United States.<ref name="intro">{{Cite web|url=https://www.cbssports.com/mma/news/ufc-ceo-dana-white-confirms-event-at-white-house-is-on-after-meeting-with-trump-administration/|title=UFC CEO Dana White confirms event at White House is on after meeting with Trump administration|publisher=cbssports.com|author=Shakiel Mahjouri|date=2025-08-29|access-date=2025-08-31|archive-date=September 6, 2025|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250906180654/https://www.cbssports.com/mma/news/ufc-ceo-dana-white-confirms-event-at-white-house-is-on-after-meeting-with-trump-administration/|url-status=live}}</ref><ref name="anotherjune">{{Cite web|url=https://thehill.com/homenews/administration/5488203-white-house-ufc-cage-match-2026/|title=White House UFC event set for June, weigh-ins at Lincoln Memorial|publisher=thehill.com|author=Elizabeth Crisp|date=2025-09-05|access-date=2025-09-05|archive-date=September 13, 2025|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250913221237/https://thehill.com/homenews/administration/5488203-white-house-ufc-cage-match-2026/|url-status=live}}</ref> The event's name is a reference to the [[United States Semiquincentennial|250th anniversary of the United States Declaration of Independence]] on the following July 4, 20 days later.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/mixed-martial-arts/articles/c743r0kl4wjo|title=Pereira to fight for third title at UFC White House|publisher=bbc.com|author=Paul Battison|date=2026-03-08|access-date=2026-03-08}}</ref> The event was initially scheduled for a July 4 date, but was later rescheduled to June 14. The rescheduled date drew criticism over its occurrence on Trump's birthday, and was described by as celebrating it more than America's.<ref name="Gelston 2026"/><ref name="McCreesh 20260615"/><ref name="Ramaswamy 2026"/><ref name="Gedeon 2026"/>

To prepare for the event, a giant structure known as "the Claw" was constructed to hold seating for roughly 4,300 people.<ref name="Ramaswamy 2026"/> No public seating was made available, with seats reserved for financial sponsors, political allies, and active-duty service members.<ref name="BBCPartner"/><ref name="att"/> The event marked the [[List of UFC records#Highest percentage of knockouts - In a single event|only UFC event to end with all fights ending by knockout]],<ref name="Stern 2026"/> and also tied events [[UFC Fight Night: Rockhold vs. Bisping]] and [[UFC on ESPN: Santos vs. Hill]] for having [[List of UFC records#Highest percentage of finishes - In a single event|all fights end by finish at a modern-era UFC event]], which had 11 and 10 fights respectively.<ref name="Hiergesell 2026"/> 

The event was broadcast exclusively on [[Paramount+]], with a reported average of 8.2 million viewers, at least 17 million unique viewers in the U.S. and Latin America, and 34 million viewers worldwide, making it the most-watched exclusive live event on Paramount+ to-date and the most watched MMA event of all time.<ref name="Viewership 1"/><ref name="Viewership 2"/> Controversy surrounded the event's use of the White House to stage cage fighting, with [[Reuters]]/[[Ipsos]] polling showing only 16% of Americans viewing the event as appropriate.<ref name="McCreesh 20260615"/><ref name="att"/> Historians described the event as unprecedented and compared it to [[gladiatorial games]] held in [[Imperial Rome]].<ref name="Weissert 2026"/> Following the event, the FBI thwarted an attempted terrorist attack targeting attendees.<ref name="Lebowitz 2026"/>

==Background==
[[File:2020 Fall Garden Tours (50506093376).jpg|thumb|left|The [[South Lawn]] of the [[White House]] held the first professional sporting event staged at the presidential residence.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/ufc/401228/dana-white-ufc-white-house-event-is-on|title=Dana White: UFC White House event 'is on'|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Mike Heck|date=2025-08-29|access-date=2025-08-31|archive-date=August 30, 2025|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250830034748/https://www.mmafighting.com/ufc/401228/dana-white-ufc-white-house-event-is-on|url-status=live}}</ref>]]

To commemorate the [[United States Semiquincentennial|250th anniversary of the United States]], the UFC planned to hold a mixed martial arts event on the South Lawn of the White House in June 2026.<ref name="intro"/> 

The event was first announced by President [[Donald Trump]] on July 3, 2025, during a rally at the [[Iowa State Fairgrounds]].<ref name="trumpiowa">{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2025/7/3/24461432/president-trump-reveals-plans-to-hold-a-ufc-event-at-the-white-house-in-2026|title=President Trump reveals plans to hold a UFC event at the White House in 2026|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Damon Martin|date=2025-07-04|access-date=2025-08-31|archive-date=September 24, 2025|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250924205735/https://www.mmafighting.com/2025/7/3/24461432/president-trump-reveals-plans-to-hold-a-ufc-event-at-the-white-house-in-2026|url-status=live}}</ref> On August 29, 2025, UFC CEO [[Dana White]] confirmed that plans for the event had been finalized, stating: "We had the meeting at the White House ... The White House fight is on."<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://sports.yahoo.com/mma/breaking-news/article/ufc-ceo-dana-white-says-white-house-card-is-officially-happening-after-meeting-with-trump-administration-035403534.html?guccounter=1|title=UFC CEO Dana White says White House card is officially happening after meeting with Trump administration|publisher=sports.yahoo.com|author=Jack Baer|date=2025-08-31|access-date=2025-08-31|archive-date=February 28, 2026|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20260228111249/https://sports.yahoo.com/mma/breaking-news/article/ufc-ceo-dana-white-says-white-house-card-is-officially-happening-after-meeting-with-trump-administration-035403534.html?guccounter=1|url-status=live}}</ref> The announcement followed Trump's remarks at the rally, where he referenced his long-standing friendship with White. Trump had frequently attended UFC events in recent years, including [[UFC 316]] in New Jersey on June 7, 2025, where several fighters greeted him cageside.<ref name="trumpiowa"/> 

While initial reports suggested a [[4th of July]] date, the event was officially rescheduled for June due to logistical reasons.<ref name="Junedate">{{Cite web|url=https://www.foxnews.com/sports/ufc-white-house-plans-revealed-including-weigh-ins-lincoln-memorial-report|title=UFC White House plans revealed, including weigh-ins at Lincoln Memorial: report|publisher=foxnews.com|author=Ryan Morik|date=2025-09-05|access-date=2025-09-05|archive-date=April 30, 2026|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20260430183752/https://www.foxnews.com/sports/ufc-white-house-plans-revealed-including-weigh-ins-lincoln-memorial-report|url-status=live}}</ref> During a speech at [[Naval Station Norfolk]] on October 6, 2025, Trump announced that it would take place on June 14, 2026, coinciding with his 80th birthday and the [[Flag Day (United States)|Flag Day]] holiday.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/story/sports/ufc/2025/10/06/donald-trump-ufc-white-house-event-date-birthday/86548457007/|title=President Trump reveals UFC White House event will happen on his 80th birthday|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Staff|date=2025-10-06|access-date=2025-10-06|archive-date=December 27, 2025|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20251227222140/https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/story/sports/ufc/2025/10/06/donald-trump-ufc-white-house-event-date-birthday/86548457007/|url-status=live}}</ref> The rescheduled date drew criticism over its occurrence on Trump's birthday three weeks before July 4, with several news outlets describing it as celebrating Trump's birthday more than America's.<ref name="Gelston 2026">{{Cite news|last=Gelston|first=Dan|date=June 15, 2026|title=UFC brings its trademark mayhem to the White House as President Trump celebrates 80th birthday|work=AP News|url=https://apnews.com/article/trump-80th-ufc-white-house-724c875d7a7cbfed087e179e8f689ec0|access-date=June 26, 2026}}</ref><ref name="McCreesh 20260615"/><ref name="Ramaswamy 2026">{{Cite news|last=Ramaswamy|first=Swapna Venugopal|date=June 15, 2026|title=Trump's UFC White House birthday bash was a bloody, on-brand affair|work=USA Today|url=https://www.usatoday.com/story/news/politics/2026/06/15/trump-ufc-birthday-white-house/90550948007/|access-date=June 26, 2026}}</ref><ref name="Gedeon 2026">{{Cite news|last=Gedeon|first=Joseph|date=June 15, 2026|title=Controversy and a corporate spectacle under the Claw: Trump’s birthday fight night at the White House|work=The Guardian|url=https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/2026/jun/15/trump-ufc-fight-white-house|access-date=June 26, 2026}}</ref><ref name="Caitlin 2026">{{Cite news|last=Tilek|first=Caitlin|date=June 8, 2026|title=Lawsuit attempts to stop UFC fight at White House on Trump's birthday|work=CBS News|url=https://www.cbsnews.com/news/white-house-ufc-fight-lawsuit-trumps-birthday-june-14/|access-date=June 10, 2026}}</ref>

The event represented the UFC's third visit to Washington, D.C., and first since [[UFC on ESPN: Overeem vs. Rozenstruik]] in 2019.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://bleacherreport.com/articles/25227329-dana-white-says-badass-ufc-white-house-event-will-be-1-1-experience-video|title=Dana White Says 'Badass' UFC White House Event Will be 'a 1-of-1 Experience' in Video|publisher=bleacherreport.com|author=Adam Wells|date=2025-07-18|access-date=2025-09-05|archive-date=September 8, 2025|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250908002222/https://bleacherreport.com/articles/25227329-dana-white-says-badass-ufc-white-house-event-will-be-1-1-experience-video|url-status=live}}</ref> The weigh-ins took place at the [[Lincoln Memorial]].<ref name="anotherjune"/> The card aired on [[Paramount+]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/ufc/461166/dana-white-reveals-broadcast-plans-for-ufc-white-house-card|title=Dana White reveals broadcast plans for UFC White House card|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Alexander K. Lee|date=2025-12-12|access-date=2025-12-12|archive-date=December 12, 2025|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20251212194631/https://www.mmafighting.com/ufc/461166/dana-white-reveals-broadcast-plans-for-ufc-white-house-card|url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/story/sports/ufc/2026/06/07/ufc-freedom-250-commentary-team-joe-rogan-white-house-broadcast/90441030007/|title=UFC Freedom 250 commentary team: Joe Rogan on call at the White House|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Mike Bohn|date=2026-06-07|access-date=2026-06-07|archive-date=June 8, 2026|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20260608023530/https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/story/sports/ufc/2026/06/07/ufc-freedom-250-commentary-team-joe-rogan-white-house-broadcast/90441030007/|url-status=live}}</ref> The event name, UFC Freedom 250, was announced during the [[UFC 326]] broadcast on March 7, 2026.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.espn.com/mma/story/_/id/48138882/ufc-white-house-card-led-topuria-gaethje-pereira-gane|title=UFC's White House card to be led by Topuria-Gaethje, Pereira-Gane|publisher=espn.com|author=Brett Okamoto|date=2026-03-07|access-date=2026-03-08|archive-date=March 8, 2026|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20260308080528/https://www.espn.com/mma/story/_/id/48138882/ufc-white-house-card-led-topuria-gaethje-pereira-gane|url-status=live}}</ref>

== Logistics and planning ==
[[File:UFC_Freedom_250_arena_at_the_White_House_Washington_DC_20260-6-08_13-36-45.jpg|thumb|The event's venue under construction at the White House South Lawn]]

In a September 2025 interview, White said the seating capacity would be under 5,000 due to security concerns, a significant reduction from Trump's earlier estimate of 20,000 to 25,000 attendees. In a February 2026 interview, [[TKO Group Holdings]] CEO [[Ari Emanuel]] stated that there would be 3,000 to 4,000 people in attendance.<ref name="tkointerview">{{Cite web|url=https://www.espn.com/mma/story/_/id/47851578/ufc-white-house-event-6-7-fights-tko-says|title=UFC White House event will have 6-7 fights, TKO says|publisher=espn.com|author=Andreas Hale|date=2026-02-06|access-date=2026-02-06|archive-date=February 10, 2026|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20260210062352/https://www.espn.com/mma/story/_/id/47851578/ufc-white-house-event-6-7-fights-tko-says|url-status=live}}</ref> White revealed in May that the capacity was expected to be 4,300.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/story/sports/ufc/2026/05/04/dana-white-ufc-freedom-250-at-white-house-tickets/89930789007/|title=Dana White reveals more about UFC Freedom 250 at White House tickets|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Josh Peter|date=2026-05-04|access-date=2026-05-05|archive-date=May 18, 2026|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20260518021718/https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/story/sports/ufc/2026/05/04/dana-white-ufc-freedom-250-at-white-house-tickets/89930789007/|url-status=live}}</ref> He also mentioned plans to install large screens at [[The Ellipse]] capable of hosting up to 85,000 spectators for a public viewing experience.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.cbssports.com/mma/news/ufc-ceo-dana-white-capacity-will-be-limited-for-event-at-white-house-but-has-plans-for-fans-to-attend-nearby/|title=UFC CEO Dana White capacity will be limited for event at White House, but has plans for fans to attend nearby|publisher=cbssports.com|author=Shakiel Mahjouri|date=2025-09-11|access-date=2025-09-11|archive-date=September 12, 2025|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250912004215/https://www.cbssports.com/mma/news/ufc-ceo-dana-white-capacity-will-be-limited-for-event-at-white-house-but-has-plans-for-fans-to-attend-nearby/|url-status=live}}</ref> White later announced that the 85,000 tickets for the viewing area would be available for free.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.espn.com/mma/story/_/id/48196436/dana-white-ufc-issue-85000-free-tickets-white-house-card|title=Dana White: UFC to issue 85,000 free tickets for White House card|publisher=espn.com|author=Brett Okamoto|date=2026-03-13|access-date=2026-03-13|archive-date=March 13, 2026|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20260313203522/https://www.espn.com/mma/story/_/id/48196436/dana-white-ufc-issue-85000-free-tickets-white-house-card|url-status=live}}</ref> The venue also held a fan fest the day before, headlined by the [[Zac Brown Band]], and featured the ceremonial weigh-ins, meet and greets, on-stage entertainment, interactive experiences, and appearances from UFC athletes and other celebrities.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/music/music-news/zac-brown-band-headline-ufc-white-house-event-concert-1236561510/|title=Zac Brown Band Will Headline UFC Event on D.C.’s Ellipse Ahead of White House Bout (Exclusive)|publisher=hollywoodreporter.com|author=Alex Weprin|date=2026-04-11|access-date=2026-04-12}}</ref>

To house the event, a 90-foot tall arch called "the Claw" was constructed around an eight-sided fighting pit known as the "Octagon".<ref name="Ramaswamy 2026"/> Further reporting described the South Lawn audience as an invite-only crowd. ''[[Time (magazine)|Time]]'' reported that at least 1,200 of the approximately 4,300 seats would be allocated to active military members, while the remaining seats would be divided among the White House, TKO Group Holdings and the UFC. Tickets to the Ellipse events remained free but required registration, while South Lawn attendees were expected to present identification.<ref name="TimeAccess">{{Cite web|url=https://time.com/article/2026/05/26/dana-white-ufc-white-house-fight-interview/|title=Inside Dana White's Plan for a White House UFC Fight|publisher=time.com|author=Sean Gregory|date=2026-05-26|access-date=2026-05-30}}</ref> In a separate interview with [[TMZ Sports]], White said Trump had 1,000 tickets, White had 200, Emanuel had 200, and that the remaining seats would go to the military.<ref name="TMZTickets">{{Cite web|url=https://www.tmz.com/2026/05/18/white-house-fight-ticket-breakdown/|title=Dana White Reveals How Many White House Tickets He & Trump Have For Freedom 250|publisher=tmz.com|author=Staff|date=2026-05-18|access-date=2026-05-30}}</ref>

On May 29, ''[[The Washington Post]]'' reported that the [[United States Department of Defense|Department of Defense]] had solicited military personnel to attend the event, with internal messages directing commands to identify junior enlisted personnel and junior officers. According to the report, personnel selected for tickets were required to pay their own travel costs, wear short-sleeve dress uniforms, and meet current waist-to-height ratio and physical fitness standards. One message reviewed by the newspaper also said tickets should be distributed to "genuine UFC fans" rather than high-ranking distinguished visitors.<ref name="WaPoMilitary">{{Cite news|url=https://www.washingtonpost.com/national-security/2026/05/29/pentagon-recruiting-troops-watch-white-house-ufc-fights-memos-show/|title=Pentagon recruiting troops to watch White House UFC fights, memos show|publisher=washingtonpost.com|author=Dan Lamothe and Alex Horton|date=2026-05-29|access-date=2026-05-30|archive-date=May 31, 2026|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20260531000431/https://www.washingtonpost.com/national-security/2026/05/29/pentagon-recruiting-troops-watch-white-house-ufc-fights-memos-show/|url-status=live}}</ref>

[[Department of Homeland Security]] officials told [[TMZ]] that the event would feature Level 1 [[Special Event Assessment Rating]] (SEAR) security measures for the card happening on the South Lawn. The fan fest event, scheduled for June 13, would also feature SEAR 1 level security. Typically events that feature that level of security have multiple agencies monitoring and assistance could include explosive detection canine teams, cyber risk assessments, venue screening, field intelligence teams, air security and tactical operations support, including a no-drone zone, ground security as well as screening and surveillance along with security checkpoints.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/ufc/483840/ufc-white-house-event-to-feature-super-bowl-level-security-from-federal-agencies|title=UFC White House event to feature Super Bowl level security from Federal agencies|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Damon Martin|date=2026-04-20|access-date=2026-04-21}}</ref>

During a fight week media day on June 10, UFC [[Chief Content Officer]] Craig Borsari spoke to reporters about possible issues with the weather and other logistical complications. Two obvious concerns raised by media, fighters, and fans alike were how the UFC planned to deal with the summer sun and heat, and the possibility of rain—or worse, a thunderstorm. He stated that "part of the reason why we picked an 8{{Nbsp}}p.m. start time was so that we could get some of the intense direct sunlight off of the canvas" and revealed they have a custom cover for the canvas, as well as a {{convert|100|ft|m|adj=on}} canopy or tarp “that also provides a lot of shade and reduces the heat quite a bit.” He also stated that light to moderate rain with no lightning will not stop the event and in the worst-case weather scenario, they have a contingency planning.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/ufc/493341/ufc-white-house-producer-details-planning-for-outdoor-elements-bad-weather-contingencies|title=UFC White House producer details planning for outdoor elements, bad weather contingencies|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Alexander K. Lee|date=2026-06-10|access-date=2026-06-10}}</ref>

===Budget and production===
[[File:P20260506MR-0725 President Donald J. Trump speaks with members of the media alongside UFC fighters in the Oval Office.jpg|thumb|right|A custom belt designed exclusively for this event was revealed on May 6 and was awarded to both title fight winners.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://bleacherreport.com/articles/25425615-new-ufc-white-house-custom-title-belt-revealed-photos-freedom-250-fight-card|title=New UFC White House Custom Title Belt Revealed in Photos for Freedom 250 Fight Card|publisher=bleacherreport.com|author=Andrew Peters|date=2026-05-06|access-date=2026-05-06}}</ref>]]

web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/ufc/460801/ufc-white-house-card-wont-have-tickets-for-sale-but-expect-spectacle-on-steroids-bigger-than-the-sphere|title=UFC White House card won’t have tickets for sale but expect ‘spectacle on steroids’ bigger than the Sphere|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Damon Martin|date=2025-12-11|access-date=2025-12-11|archive-date=December 11, In January 2026, it was reported that no taxpayer funding is being sought, and the overall expense of the event is projected to exceed the $21 million budget required to stage [[UFC 306]]'s [[Sphere (venue)|Sphere]] show in September 2024.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.sportsbusinessjournal.com/Articles/2026/01/26/white-ufc-to-pay-for-entire-white-house-card-no-taxpayer-funds-being-sought|title=White: UFC to pay for entire White House card; no taxpayer funds being sought|publisher=sportsbusinessjournal.com|author=Adam Stern|date=2026-01-26|access-date=2026-01-27|archive-date=January 28, 2026|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20260128005219/https://www.sportsbusinessjournal.com/Articles/2026/01/26/white-ufc-to-pay-for-entire-white-house-card-no-taxpayer-funds-being-sought/|url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.hitc.com/dana-white-reveals-ufc-white-house-event-costs-more-than-20-million-las-vegas-sphere-event/|title=Dana White reveals UFC White House event costs more than $20 million Las Vegas Sphere event|publisher=hitc.com|author=Aaditya Krishnamurthy|date=2026-01-23|access-date=2026-01-23|archive-date=January 24, 2026|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20260124085644/https://www.hitc.com/dana-white-reveals-ufc-white-house-event-costs-more-than-20-million-las-vegas-sphere-event/|url-status=live}}</ref> In February 2026, it was estimated the cost rose to $60 million.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://puck.news/trumps-ufc-math-problem-rob-reiners-maga-partner/|title=Trump’s UFC math problem and Rob Reiner’s ===Regulation===
[[Marc Ratner]], the UFC Vice President of Regulatory Affairs, revealed that because the White House grounds are federal property, the District of Columbia Combat Sports Commission would not regulate the event. The UFC was then expected to self‑regulate, as the [[District of Columbia Department of Parks and Recreation]] is responsible for oversight of such events within the district.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/story/sports/ufc/2026/01/30/marc-ratner-ufc-may-self-regulate-white-house-federal-land/88434643007|title=Marc Ratner: UFC may self-regulate White House card due to 'federal land'|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Mike Bohn, George Garcia and Brian Garcia|date=2026-01-30|access-date=2026-01-30|archive-date=January 31, 2026|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20260131060317/https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/story/sports/ufc/2026/01/30/marc-ratner-ufc-may-self-regulate-white-house-federal-land/88434643007/|url-status=live}}</ref>

On March 16, the District of Columbia Combat Sports Commission announced that outcomes of the fights in this event could not be recognized on the athletes’ official records as the promotion was not going to pay a $100 permit to hold the event.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.sherdog.com/news/news/DC-Commission-UFC-White-House-card-requires-permit-to-be-sanctioned-200477|title=D.C. Commission: UFC White House card requires permit to be sanctioned|publisher=sherdog.com|author=Sayan Nag|date=2026-03-16|access-date=2026-03-19}}</ref> In turn, the UFC announced that the [[Association of Boxing Commissions]] (ABC), which oversees boxing and MMA in the U.S., will be on hand to "serve as an independent third party to advise on the regulatory operations" of the event. "As the event is being held on federal property, there is no requirement for the UFC to select a state athletic commission to oversee the event," ABC president Timothy Shipman said in a release. "The UFC expresses its commitment to ensuring that this event is among the most thoroughly regulated in the history of the sport and has requested that the ABC serve as an independent third party to assist in assembling the most qualified group of judges, referees and inspectors in the world."<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/story/sports/ufc/2026/03/19/ufc-white-house-fights-will-be-sanctioned-regulated-by-association-boxing-comissions-abc-mma/89224465007/|title=UFC White House fights will be sanctioned, regulated by ABC|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Staff|date=2026-03-19|access-date=2026-03-19|archive-date=March 20, 2026|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20260320062000/https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/story/sports/ufc/2026/03/19/ufc-white-house-fights-will-be-sanctioned-regulated-by-association-boxing-comissions-abc-mma/89224465007/|url-status=live}}</ref>

===Entertainment===
Country singer [[Zac Brown]] performed the [[The Star-Spangled Banner|U.S. national anthem]] at the event.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/ufc/483806/dana-white-reveals-who-will-perform-national-anthem-at-ufc-white-house|title=Dana White reveals who will perform national anthem at UFC White House|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Mike Heck|date=2026-04-20|access-date=2026-04-21|archive-date=April 21, 2026|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20260421054549/https://www.mmafighting.com/ufc/483806/dana-white-reveals-who-will-perform-national-anthem-at-ufc-white-house|url-status=live}}</ref> This marked the first time since [[UFC 33]] in September 2001 that the promotion planned for the national anthem to be performed during a fight card.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://bloodyelbow.com/2026/04/20/dana-white-on-the-thing-that-drives-him-crazy-about-boxing-but-he-will-do-at-ufc-white-house-event/|title=Dana White on the thing that 'drives him crazy' about boxing but he will do at UFC White House event|publisher=bloodyelbow.com|author=Jamie Theodosi|date=2026-04-20|access-date=2026-06-10|archive-date=April 21, 2026|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20260421033933/https://bloodyelbow.com/2026/04/20/dana-white-on-the-thing-that-drives-him-crazy-about-boxing-but-he-will-do-at-ufc-white-house-event/|url-status=live}}</ref> The fighters entered the Octagon from various locations inside the White House, including the [[Oval Office]] for both headliners, with their walkout music performed live by the [[United States Marine Band]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://nypost.com/2026/06/14/us-news/ufc-freedom-250-card-draws-thousands-to-dc-on-trumps-80th-birthday/|title=Wild 'UFC Freedom 250' card on White House South Lawn draws rave reviews: 'Put on a great show'|publisher=nypost.com|author=Steven Nelson, Reuven Fenton and Josh Christenson|date=2026-06-15|access-date=2026-06-15}}</ref>

Unlike typical UFC events that show previews for upcoming fights and promotional videos between fights, this event played vignettes celebrating the 250th anniversary of the United States, much like the [[UFC 306]] event that took place inside [[Sphere (venue)|Sphere]] in [[Las Vegas]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/ufc/492247/ufc-plans-to-tell-the-story-of-america-in-between-fights-at-the-white-house|title=UFC plans to ‘tell the story of America’ in between fights at the White House|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Damon Martin|date=2026-06-04|access-date=2026-06-05|archive-date=June 6, 2026|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20260606115140/https://www.mmafighting.com/ufc/492247/ufc-plans-to-tell-the-story-of-america-in-between-fights-at-the-white-house|url-status=live}}</ref>

==Legal challenges==
On June 6, 2026, an emergency lawsuit was filed by the Public Integrity Project, an anti-corruption [[watchdog group]], on behalf of two plaintiffs, which aimed to block the event from occurring, describing it as a "deeply corrupt ... private, for-profit sports event".<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/2026/jun/08/trump-ufc-white-house-birthday-lawsuit|title=Lawsuit aims to block UFC event at White House on Trump’s birthday|publisher=theguardian.com|author=Joseph Gedeon|date=2026-06-08|access-date=2026-06-08}}</ref> The lawsuit challenged the transformation of the White House grounds without congressional approval and the financial benefits of the event for the UFC, Dana White, Donald Trump, and their allies. A Trump administration official responded to the suit, calling it "obstructionist" and stating that the fight card was no different than other events hosted at the White House.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/story/sports/ufc/2026/06/07/lawsuit-seeks-to-shut-down-ufc-white-house-event/90447637007/|title=Lawsuit seeks to shut down UFC White House event|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Staff|date=2026-06-07|access-date=2026-06-07}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nbcnews.com/politics/white-house/federal-lawsuit-aims-stop-ufc-event-white-house-south-lawn-rcna348910|title=Federal lawsuit aims to stop UFC event on the White House South Lawn|publisher=nbcnews.com|author=Raquel Coronell Uribe|date=2026-06-07|access-date=2026-06-08|archive-date=June 8, 2026|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20260608041308/https://www.nbcnews.com/politics/white-house/federal-lawsuit-aims-stop-ufc-event-white-house-south-lawn-rcna348910|url-status=live}}</ref> The lawsuit also highlighted that Trump purchased between $15,001 and $50,000 worth of stock in TKO Group Holdings, the UFC's parent company, in March 2026. A [[Citizens for Responsibility and Ethics in Washington]] spokesperson called it "one of the worst conflicts of interest you could imagine".<ref>{{cite news|author=Adam Edelman|date=2026-05-29|title=Trump's Stock Purchases Expose Massive Grift in UFC White House Fight|url=https://www.huffingtonpost.co.uk/entry/trump-ufc-stock-white-house-fight_n_6a19b50be4b0dade602f5c5c|publisher=huffingtonpost.co.uk|access-date=2026-06-08|archive-date=June 8, 2026|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20260608175453/https://www.huffingtonpost.co.uk/entry/trump-ufc-stock-white-house-fight_n_6a19b50be4b0dade602f5c5c|url-status=live}}</ref> White later said that they were already expecting a lawsuit, but they "thought it would be sooner".<ref>{{cite news|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/story/sports/ufc/2026/06/09/dana-white-video-comment-lawsuit-to-stop-ufc-freedom-250-was-expected-white-house/90483288007/|title=Dana White: Lawsuit to stop UFC Freedom 250 was expected|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Matt Erickson and Mike Bohn|date=2026-06-09|access-date=2026-06-09|archive-date=June 10, 2026|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20260610014804/https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/story/sports/ufc/2026/06/09/dana-white-video-comment-lawsuit-to-stop-ufc-freedom-250-was-expected-white-house/90483288007/|url-status=live}}</ref> On June 10, attorneys representing the [[National Park Service]] and [[U.S. Department of the Interior]] filed a memorandum in opposition to the plaintiffs’ emergency application for a temporary restraining order.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/story/sports/ufc/2026/06/10/ufc-white-house-justice-department-trump-response-lawsuit-stop-event-illegal-dana-white/90490204007/|title=Government fires back against lawsuit to stop UFC White House event|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Josh Peter|date=2026-06-10|access-date=2026-06-10}}</ref> On June 12, Judge [[Amit Mehta]] of the [[U.S. District Court for the District of Columbia]] refused to issue a preliminary injunction, allowing the event to proceed as planned.<ref name="Gardner and Peter 2026">{{cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/story/sports/ufc/2026/06/12/ufc-white-house-lawsuit-judges-rules-in-favor/90526176007/|title=UFC White House lawsuit: Judge rules event can be held as planned|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Steve Gardner and Josh Peter|date=2026-06-12|access-date=2026-06-13}}</ref>

==Bonus awards==
In April 2026, the UFC announced that [[Crypto.com]] would fund a US$1 million [[cryptocurrency]] bonus for the event, intended to reward the top performances on the card.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://bloodyelbow.com/2026/04/11/ufc-white-house-fighters-could-win-1-million-bonus-as-sponsor-stumps-up-for-historic-event/|title=UFC White House fighters could win $1 million bonus as sponsor stumps up for historic event|publisher=bloodyelbow.com|author=Donagh Corby|date=2026-04-11|access-date=2026-04-12|archive-date=April 12, 2026|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20260412160849/https://bloodyelbow.com/2026/04/11/ufc-white-house-fighters-could-win-1-million-bonus-as-sponsor-stumps-up-for-historic-event/|url-status=live}}</ref> The special $1 million cryptocurrency prize pool created for the event was split evenly between Justin Gaethje and Ilia Topuria.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.telecomasia.net/news/mma/topuria-and-gaethje-to-split-usd1-million-crypto-bonus/|title=Topuria and Gaethje to split $1 million crypto bonus|publisher=telecomasia.net|author=Marina Magomedova|date=2026-06-16|access-date=2026-06-16}}</ref>

On June 12, 2026, Dana White announced that the fight night awards for this event would receive a major increase as [[World Liberty Financial]] (a venture of the [[Trump family]] and the family of [[Steve Witkoff]], Trump’s friend and special envoy to the Middle East) joined as a new sponsor and added $250,000 to the bonus pool. In addition to the standard UFC amounts, "Performance of the Night" recipients earned $100,000 from the UFC, $125,000 in [[World Liberty Financial#USD1|USD1]] sponsored by World Liberty Financial, and $200,000 in CRO sponsored by Crypto.com, for a total of $425,000 each. "Fight of the Night" winners received $100,000 from the UFC along with $300,000 in CRO from Crypto.com, totaling $400,000 per athlete. The additional $25,000 UFC finish bonuses for knockouts or submissions not selected among the main post‑fight awards also remained in effect.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://heavy.com/sports/ufc/dana-white-huge-bonuses-white-house/|title=Dana White announces monster bonuses for UFC White House|publisher=heavy.com|author=Adam Martin|date=2026-06-12|access-date=2026-06-12}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmamania.com/ufc-bonuses-and-awards/451101/justin-gaethje-ufc-white-house-bonuses-staggering-825000-payout|title=Cha-ching! Justin Gaethje leads monster UFC White House bonuses with staggering $825,000 payout|publisher=mmamania.com|author=Jesse Holland|date=2026-06-15|access-date=2026-06-16}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.forbes.com/sites/brianmazique/2026/06/15/ufc-freedom-250-results-bonus-winners-highlights-and-reaction/|title=UFC Freedom 250 results: Bonus winners, highlights and reaction|publisher=forbes.com|author=Brian Mazique|date=2026-06-15|access-date=2026-06-15}}</ref>

==Aftermath==
This event marked the [[List of UFC records#Highest percentage of knockouts - In a single event|only UFC event to end with all fights ending by knockout]].<ref name="Stern 2026">{{Cite web|url=https://www.sportsbusinessjournal.com/Articles/2026/06/15/ufc-pulls-off-first-white-house-sports-event-with-freedom-250/|title=UFC pulls off first White House sports event with Freedom 250|publisher=sportsbusinessjournal.com|author=Adam Stern|date=2026-06-15|access-date=2026-06-16}}</ref> It also tied events [[UFC Fight Night: Rockhold vs. Bisping]] and [[UFC on ESPN: Santos vs. Hill]] for having [[List of UFC records#Highest percentage of finishes - In a single event|all fights end by finish at a modern-era UFC event]], which had 11 and 10 fights respectively.<ref name="Hiergesell 2026">{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmamania.com/2022/8/7/23295151/ufc-vegas-59-bonus-winners-highlights-results-espn-mma-santos-hill-usman-neal-battle|title=UFC Vegas 59 bonuses: Usman, Neal lead the way for $50K winners|publisher=mmamania.com|author=Dan Hiergesell|date=2022-08-07|access-date=2022-08-07|archive-date=December 28, 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20221228010622/https://www.mmamania.com/2022/8/7/23295151/ufc-vegas-59-bonus-winners-highlights-results-espn-mma-santos-hill-usman-neal-battle|url-status=live}}</ref>

In a post-fight interview with [[Joe Rogan]], fighter [[Josh Hokit]] repeated previously stated false claims that former [[First Lady]] [[Michelle Obama]] is [[Transvestigation|actually a man]]. His comments drew outrage throughout social media. Dana White told ''Time'' he is "completely against saying nasty and false things about people's families." However, no sanction was announced.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/story/sports/ufc/2026/06/15/ufc-white-house-josh-hokit-michelle-obama-comment/90554713007/|title=Josh Hokit's Michelle Obama comment at UFC White House sparks outrage|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Josh Peter|date=2026-06-15|access-date=2026-06-15}}</ref>

===Attempted terrorist attack===
After the event, the [[FBI]] claimed they disrupted an attempted terrorist attack with the target being attendees of the event. According to investigators, the plan involved using explosive-laden drones to strike buildings near the event, triggering an evacuation. Attendees would then be directed toward an area where snipers were positioned, followed by a planned attack on the White House gates. The FBI reportedly discovered the threat on June 10, with suspects allegedly conducting final preparations in [[Fredericksburg, Virginia]]. Numerous suspects were later taken into custody.<ref name="Lebowitz 2026">{{Cite news|last1=Lebowitz|first1=Megan|last2=Grumbach|first2=Gary|last3=Kosnar|first3=Michael|last4=Winter|first4=Tom|date=June 26, 2026|title=FBI arrests 5 in alleged plot to attack White House UFC event with explosives-laden drones and guns|work=NBC News|url=https://www.nbcnews.com/politics/trump-administration/fbi-foils-alleged-plot-attack-white-house-ufc-event-patel-says-rcna350248|access-date=June 26, 2026}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/ufc/494858/report-fbi-stopped-violent-terrorist-plot-aimed-at-ufc-white-house|title=Report: FBI stopped violent terrorist plot aimed at UFC White House, Donald Trump responds|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Alexander K. Lee|date=2026-06-15|access-date=2026-06-15}}</ref>

==Reception==
''[[The New York Times]]'' described it as a "mass-media spectacle unlike any in the history of the presidency", highlighting its use of the White House for "violent cage fighting" and eruptions of "casual cruelty" as "astonishing".<ref name="McCreesh 20260615">{{cite news|author=Shawn McCreesh|date=2026-06-15|title=For His 80th Birthday, Trump Brought a Cage Match to the White House Lawn|url=https://www.nytimes.com/2026/06/14/us/ufc-trump-white-house.html|publisher=nytimes.com|access-date=2026-06-15}}</ref> The ''[[Associated Press]]'' described the event as "[[bread and circuses]]" and interviewed historians who compared it to gladiatorial games held in [[Imperial Rome]].<ref name="Weissert 2026">{{cite news|author=Will Weissert|date=2026-06-15|title=Trump celebrates 80th birthday with an Iran deal and UFC cage fights at the White House|url=https://apnews.com/article/trump-80th-birthday-ufc-biden-e14d1bbccc1cbaaad42fd541b1fe833d|publisher=apnews.com|access-date=2026-06-15}}</ref> Conversely, [[Michael Caputo]] praised the event in ''[[The American Conservative]]'', dismissing critics as elitist "[[snob]]s".<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.theamericanconservative.com/what-the-snobs-miss-about-ufc-freedom-250/|title=What the Snobs Miss About UFC Freedom 250|publisher=theamericanconservative.com|author=Michael Caputo|date=2026-06-19|access-date=2026-06-19}}</ref> Polling conducted by [[Reuters]] and [[Ipsos]] found that only 16% of Americans found the event appropriate to hold on the White House lawn, with only a third of Republicans approving of the plan.<ref name="att"/>

===Viewership===
According to Dana White, the card brought in high numbers of viewers with reportedly 200,000 fans attending the two-day event on the Ellipse, and that viewership numbers on Paramount+ were "monstrous".<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.espn.com/mma/story/_/id/49070230/ufc-white-house-success-never-again-dana-white-says|title=UFC at White House a success, but never again, Dana White says|publisher=espn.com|author=Michael Rothstein|date=2026-06-15|access-date=2026-06-15}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmamania.com/ufc-white-house-2026-fight-card-start-time-full-results-dana-white-conor-mcgregor-cbs-mma/451009/dana-white-reveals-ufc-white-house-event-delivered-did-monstrous-numbers-on-paramount|title=Dana White reveals UFC White House event ‘delivered,’ did ‘monstrous’ ratings on Paramount+|publisher=mmamania.com|author=Ryan Harkness|date=2026-06-15|access-date=2026-06-15}}</ref> Paramount+ recorded an average of 8.2 million viewers, and at least 17 million unique viewers in the U.S. and Latin America, making it the most-watched exclusive live event on Paramount+ to-date.<ref name="Viewership 1">{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/story/sports/ufc/2026/06/18/ufc-white-house-draws-big-viewership-did-it-meet-expectations/90614524007/|title=UFC White House event draws 17 million total viewers on Paramount+|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Matthew Wells|date=2026-06-18|access-date=2026-06-18}}</ref> 

The UFC stated that updated international metrics increased total viewership for the event to an estimated 34 million, setting the domestic record for the most‑watched event in the promotion's history.<ref name="Viewership 2">{{Cite web|url=https://www.nytimes.com/athletic/7396380/2026/06/26/ufc-freedom-250-white-house-viewership-update/|title=UFC puts total Freedom 250 viewers at 34 million based on new international data|author=Matt Moret|date=2026-06-26|publisher=theathletic.com|access-date=2026-06-27}}</ref>
